sourceMovie.title: Dinosaur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Dinosaur for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Eric Leighton keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Land Before Time VII: The Stone of Cold Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dinosaur clicked for you, The Land Before Time VII: The Stone of Cold Fire (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dinosaur clicked for you, Ice Age (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dinosaur?
faq.answer: Try The Land Before Time VII: The Stone of Cold Fire, Ice Age, Moana—each shares DNA with Dinosaur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dinosaur?
faq.answer: Dinosaur (2000) is best described as Animation, Family, Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dinosaur worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
